app.name: Leksis
app.tagline: A dictionary built by its speakers.

common.poweredBy: Built on the AT Protocol — your words, your server.

errors.signInFailed: We couldn't reach that handle's server. Check the handle and try again.

hero.title: The omnilingual dictionary
hero.subtitle: Leksis is a distributed, multilingual dictionary, with a low entry barrier for the world's under-resourced languages — built one richly described word at a time.

points.own: Own your contributions — every entry lives on your own AT Protocol server, never locked inside ours.
points.universal: Built for every language — designed from the ground up for low-resource and minority languages, not retrofitted from a big one. Unilike Wiktionary, we don't have to replicate dictionary for every language, the definitions are homolingual and translations helps learners fill the gaps.
points.depth: Structure over density — one richly annotated entry is worth more than a pile of raw text. Through its complex design, the dictionary breaks the barriers between languages and between new learners and professional lexicographers. This design is thought to counter resource scarcity and to describe exhaustively the world's languages with minimum redundancy.

cta.title: Connect your PDS to start
cta.subtitle: Log in with your Bluesky or AT Protocol handle. We never see your password — authentication happens on your own server.

addLanguage.title: Add a language
addLanguage.intro: Create the language's record so words can be added to it. Anyone can improve it later — records prove authorship, not ownership.
addLanguage.tagHelp: The language's IETF (BCP 47) tag — the code used everywhere on the web to identify it.
addLanguage.syncing: Record created — waiting for the network to index it…

errors.invalidTag: This is not a well-formed language code (like "br" or "br-gw").
errors.duplicateTag: This language already exists in Leksis — pick it in the list instead.
errors.endonymRequired: Give the language's name in the language itself.
errors.writeFailed: Writing the record to your PDS failed. Try again.

search.title: Search the dictionary
search.empty: No entry matches this word yet.
search.emptyHintAll: Leksis is young — every word starts with someone adding it. Choose a language above to add this one.
search.emptyHintLanguage: Leksis is young — every word starts with someone adding it. You can be the one.
search.loadFailed: The search could not reach the dictionary. Try again shortly.
search.syncingEntry: Publishing your entry — it appears here once the network has indexed it (usually under a minute).

createEntry.title: Add “{{word}}” to {{language}}
createEntry.intro: The entry will live as a record on your own PDS; the network indexes it from there. Anyone can improve it later — records prove authorship, not ownership.
createEntry.orthographyHelp: All valid written forms — variants, archaic or regional spellings included.
createEntry.duplicateWarning_one: {{count}} existing entry already uses this spelling in this language — check it isn't the same word before publishing.
createEntry.duplicateWarning_other: {{count}} existing entries already use this spelling in this language — check they aren't the same word before publishing.
createEntry.categoriesHelp: Each category has a full form and, optionally, an abbreviation. Drag the labels to reorder them — the community's most-used tags will be suggested over time.
createEntry.todoHelp: Tasks this version still needs — one per line, so several bots or editors can each track their own. Reviewers see them on the entry; an empty list marks the entry as complete.
createEntry.todoItemPlaceholder: e.g. verify the definitions against the source
createEntry.laterFields: Translations, hierarchical structure and etymology join the entry in later milestones.
createEntry.introModify: Your proposal is published as a record on your own PDS and becomes the entry's current version. The previous version is archived, never lost.
createEntry.annotationChipHint: Drag to reorder — click for the full form
createEntry.definitionsHelp: Written in the language itself. Arrange them with the arrows: ↑ ↓ move a definition through the list (crossing group edges), → nests it one level deeper, ← brings it back up — up to three levels, numbered like a dictionary (I. 1. a.).
createEntry.notesHelp: Notes shown before this definition — “by ext.” / “by extension”, “arch.” / “archaic”…

errors.writeFailed: The entry could not be written to your PDS. Try again.

languagePage.notFound: This language does not exist (or is not indexed yet).
languagePage.loadFailed: The language could not be loaded. Try again shortly.
languagePage.todoHint: Entries whose current version carries pending work — open one to review it and clear its tasks.
languagePage.todoEmpty: No entry is waiting for review.
languagePage.todoMore: …and {{count}} more — this list shows the {{shown}} most recent.
languagePage.abbreviationsHint: Labels used across this language's entries — grammatical categories and definition notes alike — with the number of entries using each.
languagePage.abbreviationsEmpty: Nothing harvested yet — abbreviations appear as entries are published.
languagePage.namesHint: How this language names itself and how other languages name it. Add missing names — the update is published as a new version of the language record on your own PDS.
languagePage.namesSyncing: Names published — waiting for the network to index them…
languagePage.namesError: The record could not be written to your PDS. Try again.
languagePage.namesRecordUnavailable: The language record could not be read from its author's PDS, so names cannot be edited right now.
languagePage.namedInHint: Review how other languages are named here — each links to its own page.

entry.notFound: This entry does not exist (or is not indexed yet).
entry.recordGone: The entry's record could not be read from its author's PDS.
entry.loadFailed: The entry could not be loaded. Try again shortly.
entry.homonymsHint: Other entries written the same way in this language — distinct words, or possibly duplicates.
entry.conflictWarning: This label conflicts with {{pairs}} — the same form is used with a different meaning in this language.
entry.todoHint: This version asks for review — propose changes to complete these tasks, and clear the ones you finish.
entry.syncing: Publishing your proposal — the entry updates once the network has indexed it (usually under a minute).

deleteEntry.title: Delete “{{word}}”
deleteEntry.intro: This publishes a new version marking the entry as withdrawn — the previous content is archived, never lost, and the entry stays reachable at its current address to contest the deletion later.
deleteEntry.duplicateLabel: This is a duplicate of another entry
deleteEntry.reasonHelp: Required — explain why this entry is being withdrawn.
deleteEntry.reasonPlaceholder: e.g. duplicate, not an attested word…
deleteEntry.legacyNote: Anyone who already links to this entry will still be able to open it and see this reason.

errors.writeFailed: The deletion could not be written to your PDS. Try again.
